description: Post-hoc introspective gate simulation on Phase 0 Sonnet runs. Codex excluded because Codex uses Bash exclusively (no Edit/Write calls).
note: Gate thresholds were computed from Phase 0 passing runs. alignment_threshold is based on reasoning_to_action_alignment, which was RETRACTED (perm p=0.35). This means the alignment check component of the gate is built on a confounded signal.

A1_early_gate.description: Introspective gate applied to first 1/3 of trajectory only

B1_always_gate.description: Introspective gate applied to all calls

key_findings: Codex cannot be gated by this mechanism — it never uses Edit/Write tools, only Bash
B1 (always gate) blocks 92.9% of calls — too aggressive to be practical
A1 (early gate) blocks 32.5% with 62.8% precision — modest but above random (50.8% base fail rate)
Gate precision varies dramatically by repo: 76.2% on sympy (balanced), 20.0% on django (high pass rate)
Alignment check accounts for most blocks (98 alignment-only + 81 both vs 1 deliberation-only)
The alignment check is based on the RETRACTED reasoning_to_action_alignment feature
